response.justification: Deeply experienced full-stack engineer with well over the required 6 years and a strong software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). Directly relevant TypeScript/Node.js product work, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker/Kubernetes, and—notably—hands-on AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators, which maps well to the AI agent development requirement. Holds a BS in Computer Science. Main gaps are the lack of explicit Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin/Java-on-backend depth, and a frontend history weighted toward Ember.js rather than React, plus heavy investment in the niche D language. The core competencies and learning trajectory strongly outweigh these gaps.
strengths: Extensive full-stack experience with TypeScript/Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, and Docker/Kubernetes
Direct AI-agent development experience (MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks)
Strong testing/craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test libraries and ran testing workshops
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience, and frontend skews toward Ember.js over React
Heavy emphasis on niche DLang; limited evidence of Kotlin/Java backend or Python proficiency
PostgreSQL not mentioned; relational DB depth (beyond MySQL/generic SQL) unclear

key_factors.factor: Relevant core stack + AI agent experience
